title: cruciata ciliata f. laevicaulis
intro: Cruciata ciliata f. laevicaulis, also known as Marsh Peavine, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and iridoids. The health benefits associated with Marsh Peavine include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Marsh Peavine can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
